chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يُكْرَهُ لَبَنُهُ وَ مَنْ لَا يُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 30 – The one whose milk is disliked, and the one whose milk is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/76)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/76)
text: الْمَرْأَةِ يَكُونُ لَهَا الْخَادِمُ قَدْ فَجَرَتْ فَنَحْتَاجُ إِلَى لَبَنِهَا قَالَ مُرْهَا فَلْتُحَلِّلْهَا يَطِيبُ اللَّبَنُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin salim, and Jameel Bin Darraj, and sa’ad Bin Abu Khalaf,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the woman who happened to have a servant (slave) of hers who had been immoral, so we were needy for her milk. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Order her, so let her give permission, her milk is good’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Hammad ibn ‘Uthaman from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a boy who impregnated my slave-girl who then gave birth and we needed her milk. If I give permission, will her milk be fine? He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, it will be fine.’”
